failure_reason: Reviewer and judge rejected: ce_checkpoint_seq persists across view changes (UNCHANGED line 320) and can exceed new session's sequence range. MAX(backup_anchor, checkpoint_seq) produces anchor beyond recoverable range, causing MaxContiguousFrom to skip current-session archiver effects and creating permanent sequence gap. All 5 reviewer objections sustained.
technique_name: view change log merging - tighter anchor via checkpoint/backup MAX
proposal_summary: Replace either/or anchor selection in CEViewChange with MAX(backup_anchor, ce_checkpoint_seq), claiming both backup and checkpoint represent independent, composable durability sources whose maximum is always a safe anchor.
review_core_finding: ce_checkpoint_seq is NOT reset during view change (line 320: UNCHANGED <<ce_checkpoint_seq>>) and is bounded by the session-local ce_seq at checkpoint time only. After a view change, ce_seq resets to recovered+1; a stale checkpoint from a prior session can exceed the entire sequence range of the current session. Taking MAX of two quantities from different session contexts is semantically invalid and causes MaxContiguousFrom to start searching past all current-session archiver effects, yielding silent data loss.

reviewer_objections.objection: ce_checkpoint_seq not reset on view change (line 320 UNCHANGED), so it can exceed current session's sequence range
reviewer_objections.evidence: Line 320: UNCHANGED <<ce_checkpoint_seq>>. Line 281: ce_checkpoint_seq' = ce_seq - 1 only in CECheckpoint (requires ce_alive). After view change ce_seq' = recovered+1 (line 307); prior session checkpoint can exceed new session range.

reviewer_objections.objection: backup_applied and checkpoint_seq are not comparable (different session prefixes after view change)
reviewer_objections.evidence: CEArchiverSeqsForSession(ce_primary) at line 304 queries old session id. ce_backup_applied reflects post-recovery session (cleared to {} at line 312). ce_checkpoint_seq reflects pre-crash session. MAX is meaningless as a recovery anchor.

reviewer_objections.objection: Higher anchor causes MaxContiguousFrom to skip current-session recoverable effects
reviewer_objections.evidence: MaxContiguousFrom(anchor+1, available) with anchor=50 and available=session-2 archiver seqs: returns 50 immediately if seq 51 not in available. Session-3 effects at 34-35 never queried.

reviewer_objections.objection: Sequence gap: ce_seq' = 51 but session 3 only produced to 35, leaving seqs 36-50 permanently unproduced
reviewer_objections.evidence: ce_seq' = recovered+1 (line 307). recovered=50 in counterexample. Seqs 36-50 never produced. Downstream cursors stall or skip.

reviewer_objections.objection: P19 GC condition (e.seq > ce_checkpoint_seq) could GC session-3 effects that were never recovered
reviewer_objections.evidence: P19 (props.json line 171): e.seq > ce_checkpoint_seq \/ ce_archiver_ack_count < GC_K. With ce_checkpoint_seq=50, session-3 effects at 34-35 (seq <= 50) eligible for GC even if never recovered.

judgment_key_finding: Cross-session anchor comparisons are unsafe. Any proposed improvement to CEViewChange anchor must verify checkpoint_seq is bounded by the current session's sequence range, or gate on session equality (ce_checkpoint_session == ce_primary).
counterexample: Session 2: checkpoint at seq 50 (ce_checkpoint_seq=50). CE crashes. Session 3 produces seqs 26-35, backup at 33. CE crashes. Proposed: anchor=MAX(33,50)=50. MaxContiguousFrom(51, session2_archivers) returns 50. ce_seq'=51. Seqs 34-35 silently lost. Current spec: anchor=33. MaxContiguousFrom(34, session3_archivers) recovers 34-35 correctly.
